title: Patient Master File (CRUD)
description: Create, search, view patient records. Basic patient management for front-desk operations. Replaces scattered paper/Notability records.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prototype_e2e.py tests/test_prd004_features.py -k 'patient' -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "patient detail|deleted patient|patient creation"
verify: POST /prototype/patients → GET /prototype/patients → patient appears in list
verify_manual: Create patient 'John Doe' → search → patient found

title: Front Desk Operations Board
description: Room board with patient check-in/checkout, room status management, copay collection (CC), WD verified checkbox, patient signature. Replaces paper sign-in sheet + Google Sheets.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prototype_e2e.py tests/test_prd004_features.py -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "checkout collects|checkout modal has|walk-in flow|room occupied"
verify: Full workflow: checkin → assign room → service → checkout with copay/WD/sign
verify_manual: Room board shows occupied rooms, check-in/out works, copay recorded

title: Visit Management
description: Visit lifecycle tracking (checkin → in_service → service_completed → checked_out), service start/end times, room assignment.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prototype_e2e.py tests/test_clinic_workflow_e2e.py -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "walk-in flow|checkout collects|in-service|refresh keeps"
verify: POST /prototype/portal/checkin → POST /visits/{id}/start-service → visit status updates
verify_manual: Check in patient → start service → status = in_service → end → service_completed

title: Document/Signature Archive (Sign-sheet PDF)
description: Generate printable per-patient sign sheet from structured visit history. PDF includes date, service type, room, copay, WD, signature fields. Replaces Notability manual archive.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_pdf_who_what_when_where.py tests/test_prd004_features.py -k 'pdf' -q
verify: GET /patients/{id}/sign-sheet.pdf → PDF generated with visit history
verify_manual: Patient with 3 visits → download PDF → all 3 visits shown in table

title: Daily Summary Report
description: Auto-generate end-of-day report with visit counts, copay totals, staff hours. Replaces manual tallying (⑱ from tool map).
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prd004_features.py -k 'daily_summary or copay_total' -q
verify: POST /prototype/reports/daily/generate → report created with accurate totals
verify_manual: Run full day flow → generate report → totals match actual activity

title: Supabase REST API Backend
description: Production-ready backend using Supabase REST API (bypassing SQLAlchemy TCP for Vercel compatibility). Deployed to https://clinicos-psi.vercel.app.
verify: curl https://clinicos-psi.vercel.app/health → 200 OK
verify_manual: Production deployment works, all CRUD operations functional

title: Phase 1: PDF & Checkout Fixes
description: Simplified PDF format (removed WHO/WHAT/WHEN/WHERE header and staff column), fixed checkout flow, added PDF download button in checkout modal. Built on top of ROADMAP-P1-04.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_pdf_who_what_when_where.py tests/test_prd004_features.py -q
verify: Open checkout modal → verify PDF download button → click and verify PDF format is simplified
verify_manual: Full workflow: checkin → service → checkout → download PDF → verify no Staff column

title: Phase 2: Multiple Treatments UI
description: Add/edit/delete treatments on active visits, show treatments in checkout modal, treatment buttons on in_service and service_completed visits. Implements 个人诊疗记录表 (Multi-modality Treatment Record, P1 priority from roadmap).
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "treatment modality|treatment records tab"
verify: Start service → click '+Tx' → add 2 treatments → edit duration → delete one → checkout shows remaining treatment
verify_manual: Ops Board → Active Visits → in_service visit → '+Tx' button exists → works

title: Phase 3: Treatment Records Page
description: New 'Treatments' tab with date/patient/staff filters, displays all treatments across all visits with therapist/modality/duration/room
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -k 'treatment_records' -q
verify: Navigate to Treatments tab → set date filter → verify treatments appear → staff filter works
verify_manual: Add treatments to 2 visits → go to Treatments tab → see both treatments listed

title: Phase 4: Selective PDF Generation
description: Checkboxes on visit history table, 'Select All' checkbox, 'Selected PDF' button enabled when >=1 selected, backend accepts visit_ids parameter
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_pdf_who_what_when_where.py -q
verify: Patient modal → check 2 visits → click 'Selected (2) PDF' → verify PDF contains only those 2 visits
verify_manual: Patient with 5 visits → select 3 → download → PDF has exactly 3 visits

title: PostgreSQL event_log + core events (FUTURE REFACTOR)
description: Event-sourcing migration: SQLAlchemy models for event_log table and core event types. This is a FUTURE architectural refactor to add full event-sourcing. Current system works without this using Supabase REST direct DB access.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'event_log or core_events' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: POST /prototype/portal/checkin → GET /prototype/events → event appears in list
status: DEFERRED - not required for current functionality

title: DB-backed projections (FUTURE REFACTOR)
description: Event-sourcing migration: Projection tables rebuilt from event_log. Current system works without this using direct queries.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'projection or room_board or staff_hours or daily_report' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Run full E2E flow → GET /prototype/projections/room-board → all rooms visible with correct status
status: DEFERRED - not required for current functionality

title: Auth + RBAC guards (FUTURE ENHANCEMENT)
description: JWT-based auth with role enforcement. Current system has no authentication (single-clinic use).
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'auth or rbac or login' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Call /prototype/admin/rooms without token → 401. With admin token → 200.
status: DEFERRED - not required for current single-clinic deployment

title: Admin portal UI (FUTURE ENHANCEMENT)
description: Dedicated Admin tab for room/staff CRUD. Current system uses direct DB access for setup.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'admin_portal or room_crud or staff_crud' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Open UI → Admin tab → create room 'Room 1' → appears in room board
status: DEFERRED - admin functions accessible via current UI

title: User portal — visit lifecycle flow (ALREADY IMPLEMENTED)
description: Visit lifecycle is COMPLETE via ROADMAP-P1-02 and ROADMAP-P1-03. This task was for migration to event-sourced version.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'visit_lifecycle or checkin or checkout' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Check in patient → start service → end service → checkout → patient gone from active list
status: DEFERRED - functionality complete in current architecture

title: Live dashboard — room board + staff hours (ALREADY IMPLEMENTED)
description: Room board is COMPLETE via ROADMAP-P1-02. Shows real-time occupancy. This task was for event-sourced version.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'dashboard or live_board' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Start service in Room 1 → dashboard shows Room 1 as occupied within 30s
status: DEFERRED - functionality complete in current architecture

title: API + flow tests (FUTURE - event-sourced version)
description: E2E tests for event-sourced architecture. Current system has working E2E tests in test_prototype_e2e.py.
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_prototype_e2e.py -v --tb=short
verify_manual: pytest output shows 0 failures, covers checkin/service/checkout/admin flows
status: DEFERRED - tests exist for current architecture

title: PHI/logging/RBAC compliance audit (FUTURE)
description: Formal compliance audit for event-sourced system. Current system follows PHI protection best practices (no logging of patient data).
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'compliance or phi or audit' -q --tb=short
verify_manual: Run server with DEBUG logging → grep logs for patient names/DOBs → none found
status: DEFERRED - single-clinic deployment, RBAC not required

title: Final code review + go/no-go (FUTURE)
description: Code review gate for event-sourced architecture migration.
verify: manual — reviewer produces review doc with zero BLOCKERs
verify_manual: Reviewer doc at tasks/M1-REV-01-review.md with verdict: APPROVED
status: DEFERRED - event-sourcing migration not yet scheduled

title: Multiple treatments per visit — backend
description: visit_treatments table, 5 API endpoints (add/update/delete/list/filter), event logging (TREATMENT_ADDED/UPDATED/DELETED). Supports multiple modalities per visit with different therapists and durations. COMPLETE.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -q
verify: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -q
verify_manual: POST /visits/{id}/treatments/add → GET /visits/{id}/treatments → list includes added treatment

title: Multiple treatments UI — active visits (COMPLETE - covered by P2-01)
description: This is a granular breakdown of PRD005-P2-01 which is already complete.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -q
verify: Open active visit → click Add Treatment → select E-stim → save → treatment appears in list
verify_manual: Open active visit → click Add Treatment → select E-stim → save → treatment appears in list

title: Treatment records page (COMPLETE - covered by P3-01)
description: This is a granular breakdown of PRD005-P3-01 which is already complete.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py -k 'treatment_records' -q
verify: Open Treatment Records tab → filter by date range → table shows matching treatments
verify_manual: Open Treatment Records tab → filter by date range → table shows matching treatments

title: Enhanced checkout with treatment summary (COMPLETE - covered by P2-01)
description: This is a granular breakdown of PRD005-P2-01 which is already complete.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_treatments.py tests/test_prd004_features.py -q
verify: Checkout patient → see treatment summary → check 'Generate PDF' → PDF downloaded with all treatments
verify_manual: Checkout patient → see treatment summary → check 'Generate PDF' → PDF downloaded with all treatments

title: Selective PDF generation (COMPLETE - covered by P4-01)
description: This is a granular breakdown of PRD005-P4-01 which is already complete.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_pdf_who_what_when_where.py -q
verify: Open patient detail → check 2 visits → Download Selected PDF → PDF contains only those 2 visits
verify_manual: Open patient detail → check 2 visits → Download Selected PDF → PDF contains only those 2 visits

title: Admin-managed service types
description: Allow admins to create, edit, activate, and retire service types instead of relying on hard-coded frontend lists. This makes service offerings configurable without code changes.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_service_types.py -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "admin can add service type|retiring a service type|check-in modal service dropdown"
verify: Admin creates a new service type → it appears in service-type dropdowns
verify_manual: Add service type 'Lymphatic Massage' in admin UI → reopen check-in/start-service modal → new option is available

title: Staff qualification by service type
description: Store which service types each staff member can perform. In assignment flows, users choose a service type first and then only see qualified staff for that service.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_service_types.py -k 'qualification or staff_service' -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "staff dropdown filters by service type qualification"
verify: Assign service type first → staff dropdown only includes staff linked to that service type
verify_manual: Mark Staff A = PT only, Staff B = Acupuncture only → choose PT → only Staff A is selectable

title: Add deductible_met field to insurance
description: Track how much of individual deductible has been met. Required for billing calculations. Paper insurance ledger shows 'Deductible Met (IND)' column filled out daily by hand.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN deductible_met DECIMAL(10,2); verify field shows in insurance modal
verify_manual: Open patient insurance → enter deductible met amount → save → field persists and displays correctly

title: Add out-of-pocket max and met fields
description: Track annual out-of-pocket maximum (oop_max) and amount met (oop_met). Critical for billing decisions when patient hits OOP limit. Paper ledger shows 'OOP MAX' and 'OOP MET' columns.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN oop_max, oop_met; verify both fields in insurance modal
verify_manual: Enter OOP max $3000, OOP met $1500 → save → values persist → UI shows remaining $1500

title: Add coverage percentage field
description: Store insurance coverage percentage (e.g., 80%, 70%). Determines how much insurance pays vs patient responsibility after deductible met. Paper ledger shows '% after deductible' column.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN coverage_pct INTEGER; verify field in insurance modal with % display
verify_manual: Set coverage 80% → save → checkout modal calculates: $100 service = $20 patient responsibility

title: Add pre-authorization required flag
description: Flag indicating if services require pre-authorization. Staff must verify auth number before providing service. Paper ledger has 'Preauth Req?' checkbox.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN preauth_required BOOLEAN; verify checkbox in insurance modal
verify_manual: Check preauth_required → start service without auth number → system warns/blocks service

title: Add referral required flag
description: Flag indicating if services require physician referral. Must have referral on file before service. Paper ledger has 'Referral Req?' checkbox.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN referral_required BOOLEAN; verify checkbox in insurance modal
verify_manual: Check referral_required → start service without referral → system warns/blocks service

title: Add insurance effective date range
description: Store policy effective_date_start and effective_date_end. Verify coverage is active on date of service. Paper ledger shows 'Eff Date' and 'Term Date' columns.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN effective_date_start, effective_date_end DATE; verify both in insurance modal
verify_manual: Set eff dates 2026-01-01 to 2026-12-31 → attempt service on 2027-01-01 → system warns coverage expired

title: Add insurance plan code field
description: Store plan_code for insurance plan identification (e.g., 'EPO', 'PPO Gold', 'HMO Basic'). Used for billing and verification. Paper ledger shows 'Plan' column.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN plan_code VARCHAR(50); verify field in insurance modal
verify_manual: Enter plan_code 'PPO Gold Plus' → save → field displays in patient insurance summary

title: Add coinsurance field (alternative to copay)
description: Store coinsurance percentage (patient responsibility after deductible, alternative to flat copay). Some plans use coinsurance instead of copay. Paper ledger shows 'Coins %' column.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN coinsurance INTEGER; verify field in insurance modal
verify_manual: Set coinsurance 20% (no copay) → $100 service → checkout shows $20 patient responsibility

title: Dual insurance display (Primary + Secondary)
description: Display both primary and secondary insurance side-by-side in patient detail modal. Currently only shows primary. Paper ledger has separate Primary and Secondary sections.
verify: Patient with 2 insurance policies → patient modal displays both in separate sections labeled Primary/Secondary
verify_manual: Open patient with Aetna (primary) + Medicare (secondary) → both visible with clear labels

title: Session count tracking this year
description: Display total sessions used this calendar year from insurance policy. Paper ledger shows 'Sessions This Year' handwritten count. Currently tracks visits_used but resets yearly.
verify: Add sessions_used_this_year to insurance_policies; verify increments on checkout and resets Jan 1
verify_manual: Patient with 40/year limit has 15 sessions → insurance modal shows '15 / 40 used this year (resets 2027-01-01)'

title: Link visit copay to insurance copay
description: Auto-populate visit.copay_collected from insurance_policies.copay_amount at check-in. Currently manual entry. Paper workflow: staff look up insurance ledger → enter copay.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "checkout modal pre-fills copay amount from patient insurance"
verify: Check-in patient with $35 insurance copay → checkout modal auto-fills $35 in copay field (editable)
verify_manual: Patient insurance shows copay $35 → check-in → checkout → copay field pre-filled $35

title: Pharmacy insurance fields (Rx)
description: Add rx_bin, rx_pcn, rx_grp fields for pharmacy insurance info. Not critical for clinic operations but requested on paper ledger. Phase 2 enhancement.
verify: ALTER TABLE insurance_policies ADD COLUMN rx_bin, rx_pcn, rx_grp VARCHAR(50); verify in insurance modal
verify_manual: Enter RxBIN 610020, RxPCN MCNA, RxGRP 1234567 → save → fields display correctly

title: Floor grouping in room availability board
description: Group rooms by floor (16F / 18F) in operations board. Database has room.floor field but UI shows flat list. Paper room chart has clear 16F/18F separation.
verify: Operations board groups rooms: '=== 16F ===' then 16F rooms, '=== 18F ===' then 18F rooms
verify_manual: Open Ops Board → Rooms section shows '16F' header with 1601-1609, then '18F' header with 1801-1810

title: Clarify H/A/D/P treatment columns
description: Paper treatment record (诊疗记录表) has columns H, A, D, P after treatment modalities. Currently unclear what these mean. Need user clarification: History, Assessment, Diagnosis, Plan? Or different terminology?
verify: User confirms meaning of H/A/D/P → document in PRD → add columns to treatment records table
verify_manual: Treatment records tab shows H/A/D/P columns with correct labels (pending user input)

title: Eligibility verification workflow
description: Replace manual Asana eligibility workflow. Paper process: receive name → verify eligibility (call insurance) → mark verified/denied → assign to calendar. Currently 0% digital. P0 because all new patients go through this.
verify: Create eligibility_requests table; add Eligibility tab with New/Pending/Verified/Denied states; staff can mark status
verify_manual: New patient request → staff creates eligibility task → calls insurance → marks verified → patient appears in system for scheduling

title: Digital signature archive system
description: Replace Notability paper signature archive (317 patient notebooks). Need document management system to store signed PDFs, consent forms, prescriptions. Currently all paper/iPad. Phase 2 major feature.
verify: Create documents table with patient_id, doc_type, file_url, signed_date; add Documents tab; upload signed PDF → stores in Supabase storage
verify_manual: Patient completes sign sheet → scan/upload PDF → appears in Documents tab → can view/download by date

title: User accounts and JWT authentication
description: users table (user_id, clinic_id, email, hashed_password, role, is_active, last_login). POST /auth/login {email, password} → {access_token, role, clinic_id}. POST /auth/logout. GET /auth/me. Passwords hashed with bcrypt. Tokens signed with HS256, expire after 8h. Prerequisite for all RBAC and multi-tenant work.
api_test: cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_auth_rbac.py -k 'login or me or patients_endpoint' -q
verify: POST /auth/login with valid credentials → 200 + JWT token. POST /auth/login with wrong password → 401. GET /auth/me with token → user object.
verify_manual: Open login page → enter email/password → redirected to ops board. Invalid password → error message shown.

title: Clinic owner self-registration
description: Public signup flow for a clinic owner to create their account and clinic in one step. POST /auth/register {clinic_name, clinic_slug, owner_name, email, password} → atomically creates clinics row + users row (role=admin) + returns JWT. Slug must be unique. Email must be unique. No superadmin involvement needed. This is the SaaS onboarding entry point.
api_test: cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_auth_rbac.py -k 'register_clinic' -q
verify: POST /auth/register → clinic created + admin user created + JWT returned. Second POST with same email → 409 Conflict. Same slug → 409 Conflict.
verify_manual: Open /register → fill clinic name 'Sunrise PT', slug 'sunrise', email, password → submit → logged in as admin of new empty clinic

title: Role model — admin, frontdesk, doctor
description: role enum on users table: admin (full access), frontdesk (ops board + checkout + reports), doctor (read-only ops board + own treatment records + own patient notes). Role stored in JWT claims. Role visible in user profile.
api_test: cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_auth_rbac.py -k 'service_type or frontdesk' -q
verify: Create users with each role → GET /auth/me → role field correct in all cases
verify_manual: Login as doctor → checkout button not visible. Login as frontdesk → checkout button visible. Login as admin → all tabs visible.

title: RBAC API middleware — endpoint permission matrix
description: FastAPI dependency that validates JWT and enforces role permissions per endpoint. Permission matrix: admin=all, frontdesk=checkin/checkout/patients/reports, doctor=read visits + own treatments. Returns 403 on unauthorized access.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_auth.py -k 'rbac or permission' -q
verify: Call DELETE /admin/rooms with doctor JWT → 403. Call same endpoint with admin JWT → 200.
verify_manual: Use curl with doctor token → POST /portal/checkin → 403 Forbidden

title: Role-based UI — hide/show elements by role
description: Frontend checks current user role (from /auth/me or JWT claim) and conditionally renders tabs/buttons. Doctor: sees Ops Board (read-only), Treatments (own only), no Admin tab. Frontdesk: sees Ops Board, Reports, no Admin tab. Admin: sees all tabs including Admin.
verify: Login as doctor → Admin tab hidden, checkout buttons disabled. Login as admin → all tabs visible.
verify_manual: Login as doctor → attempt to click End Service → button not visible. Login as frontdesk → button visible and functional.

title: User management admin panel
description: Admin UI to create, edit, deactivate users within their clinic. Fields: name, email, role, is_active. Deactivated users cannot log in. Admin cannot delete own account. Shows last login timestamp.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_auth.py -k 'user_management' -q
verify: POST /admin/users → user created. PATCH /admin/users/{id} → role updated. PATCH /admin/users/{id}/deactivate → login attempt returns 401.
verify_manual: Admin creates user 'Dr. Smith' with role=doctor → Dr. Smith can log in → Admin deactivates account → Dr. Smith login returns error.

title: Staff-to-user account linking
description: Optional FK: users.staff_id → staff.staff_id. Allows a doctor's login account to be linked to their staff record so treatment records, reports, and assignment dropdowns resolve to the correct person. Add user_id FK on staff table. When a doctor logs in, /auth/me returns staff_id if linked. Unlinked users still work (frontdesk has no staff record).
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_auth.py -k 'staff_link' -q
verify: Create staff 'Dr. Kim' → create user with role=doctor → link user to staff_id → GET /auth/me → returns staff_id. Treatments assigned to Dr. Kim are visible when Dr. Kim user logs in.
verify_manual: Admin links Dr. Kim's login to her staff record → Dr. Kim logs in → Treatments tab shows only her own treatments

title: Tenant/clinic data model
description: clinics table (clinic_id UUID PK, name, slug, timezone, created_at). Add clinic_id FK to: staff, rooms, patients, visits, visit_treatments, event_log, insurance_policies. Migration must backfill existing rows with a default clinic_id. All queries scoped by clinic_id.
api_test: cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_auth_rbac.py -k 'isolation' -q
verify: All core tables have clinic_id column. GET /patients returns only patients for current clinic. No cross-clinic data leakage.
verify_manual: Create 2 clinics with separate patients → login as Clinic A admin → GET /patients → only Clinic A patients returned

title: Tenant isolation middleware
description: FastAPI dependency that extracts clinic_id from authenticated user's JWT and injects it into every DB query. All service functions accept clinic_id parameter. Prevents cross-tenant data access at the service layer regardless of API input.
api_test: cd backend && python3 -m pytest tests/test_auth_rbac.py -k 'isolation' -q
verify: Authenticated as Clinic A user → attempt GET /patients/{patient_id_from_clinic_B} → 404 (not found, not 403). Clinic B data is never exposed.
verify_manual: Create patient in Clinic B. Login as Clinic A user. Attempt to fetch Clinic B patient ID → 404.

title: Tenant provisioning — create new clinic
description: Superadmin endpoint to provision a new clinic: POST /superadmin/clinics → creates clinic row, seeds default rooms if provided, creates first admin user account, returns clinic slug and admin credentials. Clinic slug used in subdomain or URL prefix.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_multitenant.py -k 'provision' -q
verify: POST /superadmin/clinics {name, slug, admin_email, admin_password} → clinic created → admin user can log in → scoped to new clinic
verify_manual: Run provisioning script for 'Sunrise Clinic' → login at /sunrise → ops board empty → create rooms → functional

title: Clinic-scoped URL routing (slug or subdomain)
description: Route requests to the correct clinic context via URL slug (/c/{slug}/*) or subdomain ({slug}.clinicos.app). Frontend reads clinic context from URL and passes it in auth flow. Allows multiple clinics to share one deployment.
verify: Navigate to /c/sunrise → login → all data scoped to sunrise clinic. Navigate to /c/westside → different data set.
verify_manual: Open /c/sunrise → log in → patients list shows Sunrise patients. Open /c/westside in new window → different patients.

title: Per-clinic settings and branding
description: Clinic-level configuration: timezone (for report date boundaries), clinic name shown in header/PDF, logo upload, business hours, service types override. Stored in clinics table. Accessible through admin settings tab.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_multitenant.py -k 'settings' -q
verify: PATCH /admin/clinic/settings {timezone, name} → GET /admin/clinic → updated values returned. PDF header shows clinic name.
verify_manual: Admin sets clinic name 'Sunrise PT' → generate sign-sheet PDF → header shows 'Sunrise PT'

title: Appointment data model + CRUD backend
description: appointments table: appointment_id, patient_id (FK), provider_id (FK to staff), appointment_date, appointment_time, appointment_type (regular|eval|reeval), status (scheduled|checked_in|no_show|cancelled), cancellation_reason, notes. Backend service functions: create_appointment, list_appointments (filter by date/patient/provider), update_appointment, cancel_appointment, mark_no_show. PATIENT_CHECKIN links appointment_id to visit when checking in. Events: APPOINTMENT_CREATED, APPOINTMENT_UPDATED.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'appointment' -q
verify: POST /prototype/appointments {patient_id, provider_id, appointment_date, appointment_time} → 201. GET /prototype/appointments?date=today → includes new appt. PATCH /prototype/appointments/{id} {status: 'cancelled'} → status updated. GET /prototype/appointments/{id}/no-show → status=no_show.

title: Appointments frontend tab — check-in, no-show, cancel actions
description: Appointments tab (#tab-appointments) shows today's appointments in a table (time, patient, provider, service, status). Action buttons per row: 'Check-In' (POST /portal/checkin with appointment_id, transitions visit to ops board), 'No-Show' (POST /appointments/{id}/no-show), 'Cancel' (POST /appointments/{id}/cancel). Status badge updates live. After check-in, patient appears in ops board visits list.
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "appointments tab can check in|appointments tab can mark no-show"
verify: Open Appointments tab → create appointment via API → row appears with 'scheduled' status. Click 'Check-In' → toast 'checked in' → row shows 'checked_in' → patient appears in Ops Board. Click 'No-Show' on another → status=no_show. Click 'Cancel' on another → status=cancelled.

title: Recurring appointment scheduling
description: Allow creating a recurring series from a single appointment: recurrence_rule (weekly|biweekly|monthly), recurrence_end_date, series_id. POST /appointments generates all occurrences up to end_date. Cancel-series endpoint deletes all future occurrences in the series. Individual occurrence can be edited without breaking the series (exception record).
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_appointments.py -k 'recur' -q
verify: POST /appointments {recurrence_rule: 'weekly', recurrence_end_date: '+8 weeks'} → 8 rows created. DELETE /appointments/{series_id}/series → all future occurrences removed.

title: Scheduling calendar view
description: Calendar view in Appointments tab: week view showing all providers as columns, time slots as rows (8am–6pm, 30-min intervals). Occupied slots highlighted by provider color. Click empty slot → pre-filled new appointment form. Drag existing appointment to reschedule. Requires GET /appointments?week=YYYY-Www returning all appointments for the week.
verify: Switch to calendar view → week grid shown with provider columns. Appointments appear at correct time slots. Click empty slot → new appointment modal opens pre-filled with that time.

title: Claim data model + lifecycle state machine
description: claims table: claim_id, visit_id (FK), patient_id (FK), insurance_id (FK), service_date, procedure_codes (JSONB array), diagnosis_codes (JSONB array), billed_amount, allowed_amount, paid_amount, patient_responsibility, status (draft|submitted|pending|paid|denied|partial|void), submitted_at, adjudicated_at, payer_claim_id, denial_reason, denial_code. Status machine enforced in service layer. Events: CLAIM_CREATED, CLAIM_SUBMITTED, CLAIM_ADJUDICATED.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_billing.py -k 'claim_model' -q
verify: POST /billing/claims {visit_id, insurance_id} → claim created in 'draft' status. PATCH /billing/claims/{id} {status: 'submitted'} → status updated, submitted_at set. PATCH status back to 'draft' from 'paid' returns 400 (invalid transition).

title: Claim generation from checkout
description: Auto-generate a draft claim when a visit is checked out with payment_status='insurance_only' or 'copay_collected'. Claim is pre-populated from visit data: service_date from check_in_time, procedure_code from service_type, diagnosis_codes from visit notes if present, billed_amount from insurance fee schedule. Staff can review and submit from Claims tab. POST /billing/claims/generate-from-visit {visit_id}.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_billing.py -k 'generate_from_visit' -q
ui_test: npx playwright test --grep "checkout generates draft claim"
verify: Checkout visit with payment_status=insurance_only → GET /billing/claims?visit_id={id} → 1 draft claim returned with correct service_date and billed_amount.

title: Denial tracking and resubmission workflow
description: When a claim is denied: store denial_code (CARC code), denial_reason text, denial_date. Increment resubmission_count. Allow correcting and resubmitting: PATCH /billing/claims/{id}/resubmit {corrected_fields}. Auto-create a Task record (task_type='claim_followup', linked to claim_id) for staff to action. Track resubmission history in claim_events table (sub-events per claim). Frontend claims table shows denial badge with reason; 'Resubmit' button opens correction form.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_billing.py -k 'denial' -q
verify: PATCH /billing/claims/{id} {status:'denied', denial_code:'CO-4', denial_reason:'Not covered'} → denial_code stored. GET /billing/claims/{id} → shows denial_code + resubmission_count=0. POST /billing/claims/{id}/resubmit {diagnosis_codes: ['M79.3']} → status='submitted', resubmission_count=1.

title: AR aging report (30/60/90/120-day buckets)
description: Accounts-receivable aging projection: GET /billing/ar-aging returns claims grouped by age bucket (0-30, 31-60, 61-90, 91-120, 120+ days since submitted_at) with total billed_amount and count per bucket, broken out by payer. Frontend AR Aging tab shows a summary table and a drill-down by payer. Claims in 90+ bucket flagged red. Export to CSV.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_billing.py -k 'ar_aging' -q
verify: Submit 3 claims with submitted_at values 15, 45, and 95 days ago. GET /billing/ar-aging → bucket 0-30: count=1, bucket 31-60: count=1, bucket 91-120: count=1. Totals sum correctly.

title: ERA/EOB remittance import and auto-post
description: Import ANSI X12 835 Electronic Remittance Advice files to auto-post payments: POST /billing/remittance/import (multipart file upload). Parser extracts claim payments, maps payer_claim_id → claim_id, posts paid_amount + patient_responsibility, transitions claim status to paid/partial/denied. Unmatched claims flagged for manual review. Frontend: upload button in Claims tab, import summary modal showing matched/unmatched counts.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/test_billing.py -k 'remittance' -q
verify: Upload valid 835 file with 2 claims → both matched and posted → GET /billing/claims shows status=paid. Upload 835 with unknown payer_claim_id → flagged as unmatched in response.

title: Payer configuration registry
description: Clinic-scoped payer_configs registry for portal scraping automation. Store payer_name, adapter_type, portal_url, encrypted credentials, and login/inquiry config. Admin-only CRUD. Foundation for all automated eligibility inquiry work.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'payer_config' -q
verify: POST /prototype/admin/payer-configs with valid payload → encrypted credentials stored, GET list returns config metadata without plaintext secrets.
verify_manual: Admin adds payer config for BCBS portal → config appears in admin list → credentials never displayed back in UI.

title: Manual eligibility inquiry
description: One-click eligibility check per insurance policy using payer portal scraping. Creates eligibility_inquiries record, runs adapter workflow, updates policy projection, and exposes status lifecycle pending → running → completed/failed.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'eligibility inquiry manual' -q
verify: POST /prototype/insurance-policies/{policy_id}/eligibility-inquiries → inquiry row created and status transitions to completed/failed with policy eligibility_status updated.
verify_manual: Open patient insurance card → click Check Eligibility → status spinner appears → result updates policy fields and verification timestamp.

title: Immutable inquiry snapshots
description: Append-only eligibility_snapshots table storing encrypted raw response or secure reference plus parsed eligibility result. Every completed inquiry creates a permanent snapshot linked to patient, policy, and inquiry.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'eligibility snapshot' -q
verify: Complete inquiry → one snapshot row inserted. Attempt UPDATE/DELETE on snapshot model is blocked by app and migration policy.
verify_manual: Run inquiry twice for same patient → patient history shows two read-only snapshots with distinct timestamps.

title: Automated periodic re-inquiry scheduler
description: Background scheduler re-verifies active insurance policies at configured intervals, with retry/backoff and off-hours execution window. Replaces recurring manual Asana follow-up for stale eligibility.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'eligibility scheduler' -q
verify: Policies older than re-inquiry interval are queued automatically and retried up to max retry count when payer lookup fails.
verify_manual: Set clinic re-inquiry interval to 7 days → overnight worker queues stale policies → inquiry dashboard shows scheduled and completed runs.

title: Pre-visit eligibility trigger
description: Automatically queue eligibility verification ahead of scheduled appointments when the last verified date is stale. Designed to catch expired or denied coverage before patient check-in.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'pre_visit eligibility' -q
verify: Appointment inside 48h window with stale eligibility automatically creates a pre_visit inquiry request and surfaces result before check-in.
verify_manual: Create appointment for tomorrow with last eligibility check older than threshold → system queues inquiry and patient visit screen shows latest status.

title: Payer adapter library
description: Pluggable Playwright adapter layer for payer portals, including generic adapter plus named adapters for priority carriers. Adapters encapsulate login, member search, and eligibility extraction without changing core engine code.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'eligibility adapter' -q
verify: Generic adapter and at least one named payer adapter return standardized EligibilityResult schema for mocked fixtures.
verify_manual: Configure payer adapter type 'generic' and 'uhc' → both can run inquiries through the same engine with adapter-specific extraction paths.

title: Eligibility inquiry dashboard
description: Operational UI for pending/running/completed/failed inquiries with filters by payer, patient, trigger type, and date. Includes patient history and retry actions for failed inquiries.
verify: Eligibility dashboard lists inquiries, filters by status/payer/date, and links to patient history and snapshot detail.
verify_manual: Open Eligibility tab → see completed and failed inquiries → filter to failed BCBS runs → click patient history to inspect snapshots.

title: Eligibility alerts and patient history
description: Generate alerts when a policy changes from verified to denied/expired and show immutable inquiry history per patient. Staff can acknowledge alerts after review.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'eligibility alert' -q
verify: Eligibility status change from verified to denied produces alert row and patient history entry; acknowledgment updates alert state without mutating snapshots.
verify_manual: Automated re-inquiry returns denied after prior verified result → alert badge appears in dashboard and patient detail.

title: Clinic billing configuration
description: Clinic-scoped billing identity record for claims work: legal billing name, address, phone, tax ID, billing NPI, taxonomy, default POS, and payer-specific submitter IDs. Required before CMS-1500 assembly can succeed.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'billing config' -q
verify: POST /prototype/admin/billing-config creates clinic billing config; incomplete required fields block CMS-1500 assembly with validation errors.
verify_manual: Admin enters clinic NPI, tax ID, billing address → save succeeds and values are available to claim assembly preview.

title: Structured ICD-10 diagnosis capture
description: Support ordered diagnosis codes per visit for claim assembly, including diagnosis pointer positions needed by CMS-1500 service lines.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'visit diagnosis' -q
verify: Visit can store ordered ICD-10 codes and claim assembly returns them in CMS-1500 Box 21 order.
verify_manual: Add diagnoses M54.5 and R26.2 to visit → claim preview shows both in correct order and line diagnosis pointers reference them correctly.

title: Procedure coding on visit treatments
description: Add procedure_code, modifiers, units, charges, and rendering provider to visit_treatments so claim service lines can be assembled without manual billing lookup.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'procedure code' -q
verify: Billable treatment rows carry CPT code, units, and charge; CMS-1500 assembly emits one service line per billable treatment.
verify_manual: Visit with two treatments 97110 and 97140 → claim preview shows two 24A-24J line items with units and charges.

title: CMS-1500 assembly API
description: Normalized backend endpoint that assembles patient, insured, provider, diagnosis, and service-line data for a visit into a stable CMS-1500 JSON payload, including readiness warnings.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'cms1500 assembly' -q
verify: GET /prototype/billing/visits/{visit_id}/cms1500 returns normalized payload plus warnings/errors for missing required claim data.
verify_manual: Request claim payload for checked-out visit → response includes patient block, insurance block, provider block, diagnoses, service lines, and validation warnings.

title: Chrome extension shell (Manifest V3)
description: Build the extension runtime with popup UI, background service worker, content script, and Chrome storage wiring. This is the delivery vehicle for portal autofill.
verify: Unpacked extension loads in Chrome, popup opens, background worker runs, and content script can message the worker on a mapped portal page.
verify_manual: Load extension in Chrome developer mode → click icon → popup renders and detects current page context.

title: Extension authentication
description: Secure extension login/logout flow using ClinicOS auth. Background worker owns tokens, preserves clinic context, and never exposes bearer token into page DOM.
verify: User can authenticate from extension popup, call protected claim assembly endpoint, and is forced to re-authenticate after token expiry.
verify_manual: Login from extension popup using ClinicOS credentials → visit search loads. Logout clears extension state and further API calls fail with 401 until re-login.

title: Patient / visit claim selector
description: Popup UI to search claim-eligible visits by patient, DOS, payer, or provider and preview readiness warnings before fill. Prevents staff from filling the wrong visit on a payer portal.
verify: Extension popup lists only claim-eligible visits and displays claim warnings before autofill can start.
verify_manual: Search patient name in extension popup → select visit → preview shows payer, DOS, missing-field warnings, and ready-to-fill status.

title: Portal field mapping registry
description: Admin-managed registry of portal domains, selector sets, transform rules, and mapping versions for CMS-1500 fields. Lets the extension adapt to different payer portals without code changes per field.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'portal field mapping' -q
verify: POST mapping registry entry with domain and field_map JSON → GET active mapping returns versioned selector configuration for extension use.
verify_manual: Admin saves mapping for payer portal domain → extension on that domain resolves active mapping version before fill.

title: Auto-fill execution engine
description: Content-script engine that applies mapping rules to portal DOM, fills CMS-1500 fields, highlights failures, and stops before final submit for user review.
verify: Extension fills mapped CMS-1500 fields on a test portal page, reports failed selectors, and never clicks final submit automatically.
verify_manual: Open mapped payer claim page → choose visit → click Fill → visible fields populate and any failed fields are highlighted for manual correction.

title: Claim fill audit trail
description: Audit every claim assembly and extension fill attempt with actor, visit, portal domain, mapping version, and outcome. Supports compliance review and debugging when portal mappings drift.
api_test: cd backend && python -m pytest tests/ -k 'claim fill audit' -q
verify: Every extension-initiated fill attempt creates audit record with outcome, field counts, and mapping version, without storing raw PHI in event payloads.
verify_manual: Run autofill on test portal → claim fill audit screen shows visit ID, user, portal, success/failure status, and mapping version.
